Municipality will be properly indemnified.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should always see to it that: No Additional cash advances are granted to officials and employees with outstanding balances; Elected officials are not granted cash advances aside from official travel; Cash advances are reported as soon as the purpose/s for which these were given have been served; Cash advances are liquidated before the end of the year; Agency clearances are not signed unless any unsettled accounts/ outstanding cash advances  are paid or  deducted from whatever money claim they have from the Municipal Government; Only bonded accountable officers are granted cash advances; Withholding of salaries for personnel with outstanding cash advances, while for those who are already deceased, write-off of receivables may be considered. We likewise recommended to require all accountable officers with outstanding cash advances to expedite the liquidation of the remaining balances of P3,679,511.50.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the disposal of the unserviceable property.
The Municipal Accountant will coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to identify and have a complete list of land owned by the Municipality. Moreso,  concerning identifying, registration and titling of land properties supposedly owned by the municipality, Municipal Assessor was directed to validate and immediately carry out what is being recommended.
The Municipal Accountant commented that they will issue final demand letters and/or seek authority to withhold any amount due to the employees who have outstanding unliquidated cash advances.
Moreover, he added that regarding the separated employees, they will apply the amounts due to them against their advances and they will also secure approval to write-off those deceased employees.   In addition, they already have requested for the increase of bonded employees and will authorize only bonded employees be granted  cash advances.
observations.reason: Additional cash advances still granted to officials and employees with outstanding balances; Elected officials are still granted cash advances aside from official travel; Cash advances were not reported as soon as the purpose/s for which these were given have been served; Cash advances were not liquidated before the end of the year; Only bonded accountable officers are not yet all in place; Withholding of salaries and writing-off of receivables not fully undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant see to it that the appropriate/specific expense accounts are used in the recording and charging of transactions incurred by the Municipality for fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the foods/meals to be served during special activities of the municipality should be included and clearly specified in the breakdown of expenses to be incurred in a particular program or activity.
We likewise recommended for the submission of a legal basis for the grant of additional quarterly allowance of P2,000.00 to the Liga ng mga Barangay (LNB), composed of 64 Punong Barangays, totaling P512,000.00 for Calendar Year 2015.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Budget Officer commented that they had discontinued issuing obligations/charging to Other MOOE accounts for transactions that have particular appropriate/specific accounts.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant commented that expense reclassification shall be made to ascertain that specific expense accounts are used in recording and charging of LGU’s transactions.
The management will make query with DBM.
observations.reason: CY 2016 still showed erroneous charging to Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expense account. Legal basis not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC-General Services Officer to see to it that all insurable properties are covered with sufficient insurance coverage with the GSIS for protection against any damage to, or loss of their properties and avoid the risk of non-recovery of asset values if man-made or fortuitous events occur.
observations.reason: Still no insurance coverage on the insurable properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Treasurer to seek the approval of the Department of Finance to continue maintaining deposits with PNB - Silang, Cavite or coordinate with concerned municipal officials to facilitate the transfer of the funds from PNB to LBP or DBP.
observations.managementAction: Had advised concerned municipal officials to facilitate the transfer of the funds from PNB to LBP or DBP.
observations.reason: Still with deposit with the Philippine National Bank, Silang, Cavite.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain seven (7) sets of complete subsidiary ledgers for the special accounts, loan and 20% Development Fund to facilitate the preparation of financial reports and timely evaluation of their operations.
Relative thereto, secure a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open separate accounts for each special accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant commented that they used to keep subsidiary ledgers for special accounts for various economic enterprises on manual basis.  However, with the introduction of automation system, recording of same was omitted.
The Municipal Mayor had instructed the Treasury, Budget and Accounting Offices to immediately carry-out the recommendation.
observations.reason: Still not maintaining special accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the: Inventory Committee facilitate the completion of the inventory taking of all PPE of the Municipality and prepare the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer prepare and maintain real property ledger cards and perpetual inventory record of properties; OIC–General Services Officer maintain a complete file of Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PAR) and have it renewed every three years; Disposal Committee conduct inventory of all unserviceable PPEs for disposal and ensure that the proper disposal of the same is strictly observed. Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the disposal of the unserviceable property.
observations.managementAction:
Inventory Committee (IC) spearheaded by GSO was created specifically to conduct on regular basis physical inventory of all PPE.  Composed of different departments’ staffs, the IC is responsible to handle/take charge of the physical count of the LGU’s properties.
The committee has already started the physical inventory yet  was not able to complete the task due to time and manpower constraints. Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer prepare and maintain real property ledger cards and perpetual inventory record of properties; No PPE ledger cards being maintained by the accounting office. Property cards maintained by the GSO only on selected PPE accounts. Disposal Committee conduct inventory of all unserviceable PPEs for disposal and ensure that the proper disposal of the same is strictly observed; No action taken by the Agency on proper disposal of unserviceable PPE of the municipality.
observations.reason: Physical inventory report pertains only to some PPE accounts. The agency reasoned out lack of personnel to facilitate the completion of PPE inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the LGU Authorized Representative for the Municipal Computerization System submit immediately the essential information/documentation necessary for the timely auditorial review and post-audit of the payments for the contracts with Lexsys Technologies, Inc.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that all the required supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before final payment to facilitate review of the validity/propriety of the disbursements and avoid possible audit suspensions and/or disallowances.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant had submitted previously paid disbursement vouchers pertaining to contracts with Lexys under progress payment mode.
Treasury was directed by the Municipal Mayor to request for a meeting with Lexsys Technologies Inc. to immediately resolve the matter at hand and had stopped further payments because of these unresolved issues and concerns of different end-users.  Further stressed-out that they will require Lexsys Technologies Inc. to assign capable personnel who are always available to attend to each end-users queries; if so required,  incorporate to the system programs needed revisions/additions necessary for the  end-users’ daily  operation and;  turn-over the system program to the municipality’s in-house IT personnel and teaching them the complete program procedures in its operation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), General Services Office and/or the Municipal Engineering Office, as the case may be to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts/Purchase Orders and all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five days from its execution as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Made arrangement with BAC and GSO to assign a personnel who will handle submission of all approved contracts every end of the week to the office of the auditor. Beginning of CY 2016, have been complying to the recommendation as to submission of POs and contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor and Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and consider possible strategies to intensify collections of RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: Had coordinated with Assessor and Treasury to provide a more accurate amount to be provided as receivables in the beginning of the year to ensure the precise outstanding balances of these receivables.
observations.reason: With erroneous setting-up of RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Cooperative Development Officer in retrieving copy of loan agreements, data of the debtors and records of payments.
observations.managementAction: MCDC which handled the granting of loan to cooperatives has been making follow ups and demand payments through letters to facilitate collection. Those who failed to comply with their monthly obligations were all given due notices reminding them of their past due loans. However, LGU is willing to condone all the interest, surcharges and penalties therein to enforce and relieve them of loan payments. For those no longer existing and non-paying cooperatives, we will seek authority from COA to write-off their loan obligations.
observations.reason: MCDC which handled the granting of loan to cooperatives has been making follow ups and demand payments through letters to facilitate collection. Those who failed to comply with their monthly obligations were all given due notices reminding them of their past due loans. However, LGU is willing to condone all the interest, surcharges and penalties therein to enforce and relieve them of loan payments. For those no longer existing and non-paying cooperatives, we will seek authority from COA to write-off their loan obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat to ensure that all documents supporting the eligibility checklists are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers to facilitate evaluation of the legal personality and capacity of all participating and winning bidders.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were attached to the disbursement vouchers. Required documents are attached to the disbursement voucher documentation prior to the transactions approval/payment.
observations.reason: BAC was mandated to comply by including the eligibility checklist as part of disbursement voucher documentations prior to the transactions approval/payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to see to it that all the Programs/Projects/Activities of the municipality are publicized in the prescribed form of tarpaulin signboards as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Engineering has been advised to strictly comply with COA’s recommendation on to publicize all projects/activities through public notification in the prescribed form of tarpaulin signboards as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004. To assure compliance, public notification shall form of the required documentation for all future infrastructure projects before its implementation/execution.
observations.reason: Only projects of significant amount/value were publicized by engineering through tarpaulin signboards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to see to it that compliance with the Segregation at Source/Segregated Collection and in its Disposal Facility is strictly observed to avoid possible penalties provided for under RA 9003.
observations.reason: Agency reasoned out that information drive regarding proper segregation, collection and disposal is still on the planning stage; lack of garbage collection trucks and; right of way issues that impedes the installation of 3-phase electrical connections necessary to run the material recovery facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the payments of salaries, wages and other benefits thru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) instead of manual payments through cash advance of the designated Disbursing Officer to address the risk of possible loss of funds.
observations.managementAction: Have shifted from manual to automation by adopting the use of ATM from LBP for  payment of salaries, wages and benefits of LGUs regular and casual employees.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned department heads to: 1. limit the procurement of goods and services on a reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application; 2. see to it that the documentary requirements such Certificates of Emergency Purchase and completely dated and filled-up Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR), Report of Waste Materials, Pre-Repair Inspection Report and Summary of Expenses are attached to the Disbursement Voucher (DV), where applicable; 3. secure at least three (3) price quotation from known suppliers if reimbursement could not be avoided.
observations.managementAction: Had advised concerned department heads to limit procurements on reimbursements basis. Had see to it that all the required documentary requirements are attached prior to payment. Secured at least three (3) price quotations from suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC Municipal Environment and natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to expedite the implementation of the contract with the accredited Sanitary landfill immediately.
observations.managementAction: Opted to engage the services of outside contractor to cater for their needs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and GAD Focal Person to see to it that all programs, projects and activities included in the GAD Plan are supportive of the gender issues in the Municipality as required under RA No. 7192.
We likewise recommended evaluation of the non-GAD related disbursements for possible charges to the appropriate fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Submitted Annual Gender and Development Plan and Budget for CY 2015 were reviewed and approved by DILG. Integrated therein were gender issues, causes and results including GAD activities.
